tenders.title: MoRTH Model Request for Proposal (RFP) for Consultancy Services — QCBS
tenders.scopeSummary: MoRTH model RFP that road authorities adapt for consultancy assignments. Defines the Tender Information Summary (TIS), consultant forms (T-2 organisation & experience, T-2A technical capacity, T-3 comments on ToR, T-4 approach/methodology/work plan, T-5 work schedule, T-6 team composition & CVs), Section VII scoring criteria, QCBS as the default selection method, and mandatory INFRACON registration with a unique Team ID.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Defined per assignment in TIS; typically similar-nature consultancy in last 5–7 years
keyPersonnel: Key Experts defined per assignment ToR
other: Mandatory INFRACON registration of firm and all key personnel
Unique INFRACON Team ID quoted with bid
Bid via eprocure.gov.in

technicalCriteria.criterion: Consultant's relevant experience & past performance (Form T-2/T-2A)
technicalCriteria.notes: Weight set per assignment in Section VII

technicalCriteria.criterion: Approach, methodology & work plan (Form T-4)
Qualifications & experience of Key Experts (Form T-6 + CVs)
technicalCriteria.notes: Usually the largest weight; Team Leader gate common

tenders.title: PMGSY Quality Monitoring & Mandatory Road Safety Audit (Rural Roads)
tenders.issuingAuthority: NRIDA (National Rural Infrastructure Development Agency), Ministry of Rural Development
tenders.scopeSummary: PMGSY operates a three-tier quality management mechanism; the third tier is independent National Quality Monitors (NQMs) — empanelled retired senior engineers engaged by NRIDA to inspect and test rural road works on a random-sampling basis. Additionally, a Road Safety Audit is mandatory at the design stage for all PMGSY-III roads longer than 5 km, creating demand for road-safety-audit consultancies.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: NQM empanelment requires senior engineering experience (State/Central organisations); road safety audit requires certified safety auditors
other: Empanelment model (individuals) rather than firm QCBS for NQMs
Road Safety Audit mandatory for PMGSY-III roads >5 km per Supreme Court Committee on Road Safety

technicalCriteria.criterion: Engineering seniority & domain experience (NQM empanelment)
Road safety audit certification & experience

tenders.sourceDocument: NRIDA/PMGSY Guidelines on Quality Assurance & Control Mechanism; Performance Evaluation of NQMs

evaluationFramework.summary:
Consultant selection in the Indian road & highways sector is highly standardised and overwhelmingly uses Quality & Cost Based Selection (QCBS) with an 80:20 technical:financial weight. A technical proposal must score at least 75 out of 100 to have its financial bid opened, and the Team Leader's CV must independently score at least 75% or the entire proposal is rejected. Both the firm and every proposed key personnel must be registered on the MoRTH INFRACON portal, which auto-validates credentials; bids quote a unique INFRACON Team ID.

Technical scoring follows two archetypes. For DPR/feasibility assignments the 100 marks split roughly as Firm experience 25, Approach & methodology 5, Survey/testing/technology (in-house lab, LiDAR, GPR) 20, and Key personnel 50. For Authority's Engineer / Independent Engineer / Supervision assignments the split is either 35/5/20/40 (NHAI O&M-IE, with a dedicated 20 marks for road-inspection technology such as NSV, FWD, MBIU and Retro-Reflectometer) or 20/20/60 (NHIDCL / multilateral-funded AE). In every archetype, key personnel and firm experience together dominate, while approach & methodology is a small share.

Firm experience is credited relative to the project length (aggregate similar-length km, plus at least one project of 40–50% of the package length, in the last 7 years) and adjusted by the firm's role (sole 100%, lead JV 75%, other JV 50%, associate 25%). Turnover generally must average at least ₹5 crore from consultancy business, with more marks for higher turnover. Length-equivalence (4-lane counted ×1.5, feasibility counted as ⅔ of DPR) and year-wise enhancement factors are commonly applied.

criteria.label: Firm's Relevant Experience
criteria.description: Track record of similar road consultancy assignments (DPR/feasibility for DPR tenders; AE/IE/supervision for supervision tenders), measured by aggregate km, number of qualifying projects, major-bridge assignments, and multilateral-funded experience. Credit is weighted by the firm's role (sole/lead JV/other JV/associate) and by length-equivalence factors.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Aggregate similar length > 3x package length AND 3+ qualifying projects each >=40-50% of package length (or 3+ AE/IE assignments), some multilateral-funded
Aggregate length > 1-2x package length AND 2 qualifying projects; solid recent track record
Meets the minimum floor: aggregate >= package length and 1 qualifying project of >=40-50% length
Below the eligibility floor or experience mostly as minor JV partner/associate (25-50% credit)

inputFields.label: Number of similar assignments completed in last 7 years (DPR or AE/IE, matching the tender type)
inputFields.scoring: 1 project = minimum/Fair; 2 = Good; 3+ = Excellent

inputFields.label: Aggregate length of similar road consultancy (km, 4-lane counted x1.5, feasibility x2/3)
inputFields.scoring: >=1x package length = Fair; >2x = Good; >3x = Excellent

inputFields.label: Number of DPRs for major bridges (>200 m) completed (DPR tenders only)
inputFields.scoring: 1 bridge = 2 marks up to 4+ bridges = 5 marks (DPR archetype)

inputFields.label: Experience on World Bank / ADB / JICA-funded road projects?
inputFields.scoring: Adds up to 3 marks in NHIDCL/multilateral AE tenders

inputFields.label: Typical role of the firm in cited assignments
inputFields.scoring: Experience credit multiplied by the selected role weight

criteria.label: Average Annual Turnover (Consultancy)
criteria.description: Average annual turnover from consultancy business over the last 3-5 years, certified by a Chartered Accountant. Acts as both an eligibility gate (typically >=₹5 Cr) and a scored sub-criterion within firm experience.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Average annual consultancy turnover >= ₹50-60 Cr
Average annual turnover ₹20-50 Cr
Average annual turnover ₹10-20 Cr
Average annual turnover ₹5-10 Cr (meets floor); below ₹5 Cr = ineligible

inputFields.label: Average annual turnover from consultancy business (last 5 years, ₹)
inputFields.scoring: >=₹5 Cr required; marks scale up through ₹10 / ₹30 / ₹60 Cr bands (e.g. 0.5 mark at 5-10 Cr up to full marks above 50 Cr)

criteria.label: Approach, Methodology & Work Plan
criteria.description: Quality of site appreciation, comments/suggestions on the ToR, proposed methodology and work plan, team composition and organisation. Small weight (5 marks) in NHAI/MoRTH RFPs but larger (up to 20 marks) in NHIDCL/multilateral AE RFPs.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Sharp site appreciation, innovative but realistic methodology, clear work plan and staffing, strong understanding of key challenges
Sound methodology and work plan with minor gaps
Generic methodology largely restating the ToR
Weak or boilerplate approach, poor understanding of the assignment

inputFields.label: Self-assessed quality of your approach & methodology
inputFields.scoring: Maps to the scoring guidance levels; typically 5 marks (NHAI/MoRTH) or up to 20 marks (NHIDCL/JICA)

inputFields.label: Have you conducted a site visit / site appreciation for this project?
inputFields.scoring: Site appreciation is an explicit sub-mark (~1-2 marks)

criteria.label: Survey, Testing & Inspection Technology
criteria.description: For DPR: in-house material testing lab, field investigation facilities, office equipment/software, and experience with LiDAR-based survey, GPR/induction locators for utility detection, and cadastral map digitisation. For O&M/IE: capability in Network Survey Vehicle (NSV), Falling Weight Deflectometer (FWD), Mobile Bridge Inspection Unit (MBIU) and Retro-Reflectometer. Worth ~20 marks where applicable.

scoringGuidance.meaning: In-house lab + field facilities + software, and 4+ projects using LiDAR/GPR (DPR) or in-house NSV/FWD/MBIU/Retro-Reflectometer (O&M/IE)
Mostly in-house capability with 2-3 technology projects
Key facilities outsourced (half marks) with 1 technology project
Facilities not available and no advanced-technology experience

inputFields.label: Material testing facility availability
inputFields.scoring: In-house = full (~2 marks), outsourced = half, none = 0 (DPR archetype)

inputFields.label: Number of projects using LiDAR / equivalent for topographic survey
inputFields.scoring: 1 project = 1 mark up to 4+ = 4 marks (DPR)

inputFields.label: Number of projects using GPR / induction locator for subsurface utility detection
inputFields.scoring: 1 = 1 mark up to 4+ = 4 marks (DPR)

inputFields.label: Road-inspection equipment available (O&M/IE tenders)
inputFields.scoring: Up to 20 marks; associate for equipment must not be shared with another bidder

criteria.label: Qualifications & Competence of Key Personnel
criteria.description: The largest scoring block (40-60 marks). Each key expert is scored on general/essential qualification, total and relevant experience, experience in similar capacity, and tenure with the firm. The Team Leader is gated: their CV must score >=75% or the whole proposal fails. Missing essential education = zero for that CV; duplicate CVs across firms = zero. All CVs must be validated on INFRACON.

scoringGuidance.meaning: All key experts exceed essential qualifications with extensive similar-capacity experience on comparable NH/PPP projects; Team Leader well above 75%
Full team meets requirements; Team Leader clears 75%; most experts with strong similar experience
Team meets minimum qualifications; Team Leader just clears 75%; some experts light on similar-capacity experience
One or more essential positions unfilled/under-qualified, or Team Leader below 75% (disqualifying)

inputFields.label: Team Leader — total professional experience (years)
inputFields.scoring: Scored under total experience + similar-capacity; must yield >=75% CV score to avoid disqualification

inputFields.label: Team Leader — number of similar assignments (as TL / senior role)
inputFields.scoring: Drives 'experience in similar capacity' (largest CV sub-block)

inputFields.label: Does the Team Leader meet the essential educational qualification in the ToR?
inputFields.scoring: If No, CV = 0 marks and proposal fails

inputFields.label: Number of required key-expert positions you can fill with qualified CVs
inputFields.scoring: DPR needs ~8 experts (TL, Bridge, Highway/Pavement, Geotech, Traffic/Safety, Survey, Environment, QS); AE/IE ~4 (TL, Resident/Highway, Bridge, Road Safety)

inputFields.label: Average tenure of proposed key experts with your firm (years)
inputFields.scoring: >2 yrs = full tenure marks (~10 per CV), 1-2 yrs = partial, <1 yr = 0

inputFields.label: Do key experts (TL, Bridge, Road Safety) have PPP/HAM project experience?
inputFields.scoring: PPP-experienced personnel are explicitly rated higher

criteria.label: INFRACON & Statutory Compliance
criteria.description: Mandatory pass/fail compliance: the firm and every proposed key personnel must be registered on INFRACON, form a Team, and quote a unique INFRACON Team ID; bid submitted via eProcure. No debarment by MoRTH/agencies in the last 2 years; no conflict of interest with the concessionaire/contractor.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Firm + all key personnel registered on INFRACON with Team ID; no debarment; no conflict of interest
Registered but minor CV/profile updates pending on INFRACON
Registration incomplete for some personnel (must be fixed before submission)
Firm or key personnel not registered, or active debarment/conflict of interest (bid rejected)

inputFields.label: Are the firm and all proposed key personnel registered on INFRACON (with Team ID)?
inputFields.scoring: Mandatory; non-registration leads to rejection

inputFields.label: Any debarment/penalty by MoRTH or its agencies in the last 2 years?
inputFields.scoring: If Yes, ineligible

inputFields.label: Any conflict of interest with the project concessionaire/contractor?
inputFields.scoring: If Yes, ineligible for that assignment

keyPolicies.name: Manual for Procurement of Consultancy & Other Services (MoF, Dept. of Expenditure, updated June 2022)
keyPolicies.relevance: Umbrella policy under GFR 2017 defining consultant selection systems (QCBS, LCS, SSS, QBS/FBS) that road-sector RFPs implement.

keyPolicies.name: MoRTH Model RFP for Consultancy Services (QCBS)
keyPolicies.relevance: Sector-standard RFP: Tender Information Summary, Forms T-2 to T-6, Section VII scoring, QCBS default, mandatory INFRACON linkage.

keyPolicies.name: INFRACON — National Portal for Infrastructure Consultancy Firms & Key Personnel
keyPolicies.relevance: Mandatory credential repository/validation for firms and key personnel across MoRTH/NHAI/NHIDCL/state NH agencies; unique Team ID quoted with every bid.

keyPolicies.name: NRIDA/PMGSY — Guidelines on Quality Assurance & Control Mechanism
keyPolicies.relevance: Rural-roads quality regime: three-tier quality monitoring (NQMs) and mandatory Road Safety Audit for PMGSY-III roads >5 km.
